titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Polyglot Press Collection
titlestmt.author: Compiled by ALSC staff

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 12001217.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday September 1, 2020 at 23:00

did.unittitle: Polyglot Press collection
did.abstract: The collection contains a selection of works printed by Polyglot Press, many of which reproduce illustrations by Fritz Kredel (1900-1973), a German graphic designer and illustrator active in the United States.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the proprietor of Polyglot Press, Arthur Graham.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Polyglot Press Collection, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Some of the materials have been individually cataloged in Orbis.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also the Fritz Kredel Collection (Call # AOB 16).

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Arthur Graham is the proprietor of Polyglot Press in Lexington, KY. "Graham [is] a former tenor with the Metropolitan Opera and now a professor of Music at the University of Kentucky. Prof. Graham began printing in New York and continued in Pennsylvania and Florida before moving to Lexington. Printing as the Polyglot Press, he has brought fine typographic treatment to works in various languages. He has printed in Danish, in English, in French, in German, in Hebrew, in Latin, in Neapolitan, in Spanish, and in Yiddish, and his work demonstrates a sensitive appreciation for linguistic nuance. In addition to the variety of languages in which he prints, Arthur Graham also has assembled an enviable array of type fonts, allowing him to bring further suggestions of meaning and mood to the way in which his words are displayed."

Reference: Burton Milward, "The Private Press Tradition in Lexington, Kentucky," in The Kentucky Review , Volume XI, Number 3, Autumn 1992, pp. 5-27. Copyright © 1992 by the University of Kentucky Libraries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains a selection of works printed by Polyglot Press, many of which reproduce illustrations by Fritz Kredel (1900-1973), a German graphic designer and illustrator active in the United States. Many of these published works also have individual catalog records in Orbis.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Bound Volumes, 2002-2011. II. Prints, 1970-2013. III. Correspondence & Ephemera, undated.

did.unittitle: Bound Volumes

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically and alphabetically by title within each year.

did.unittitle: The Rime of the Ancient Mariner
Gesammelte Weisheit übersetzt auf englische Knüttelverse
Naughty Helen: An Excerpt from Wilhelm Busch's Die Fromme Helene
Mein Volk by Else Lasker-Schüler with Sketches of the Poet by Miron Sima
Poetic Aphorisms from the Sinn-Gedichte of Friedrich von Logau by Henry Wadsworth Longfellow
A Polyglot Limerick: La Chevelure (original copyright 1973)
Fritz Kredel: Woodcuts from Doctor Faust
Mythologie [by] Heinrich Heine
Cement Poetry
Con & Pro: Quotations on Type Design
Hölderlin "Two Poems" with an Engraving of the Poet on Brass by Victor Hammer
did.physdesc: 3 items : 1 volume with 1 engraved portrait and 1 correction laid in

did.unittitle: Rubaiyat: A Study in Translation
Fritz Kredel Candide
Fritz Kredel Federzeichnungen [of] C.M. Wieland Die Wasserkufe oder Der Einsiedler und die Seneschallin von Aquilegia
Fritz Kredel Illustrations: The Blonde Corinthian
Fritz Kredel Woodcuts + The Gawain Poet
The Importance of Being Earnest : Fritz Kredel Drawings for Oscar Wilde's Play
Prints

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically and alphabetically by title within each year.

did.unittitle: A Poem by Franz Schubert
Urworte. Orphisch. [by] Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
Die Geschichte der Literatur ist ebenso... Heine
A Polyglot Psalter
A Psalm of David XXIII [with four serigraphs by Grace Perreiah]
Hans Sachs - Jost Amman Two Poems from Exact Description of All Ranks on Earth [1568] with the original woodcuts and translated by Arthur Graham
John Tuska: Eight Drawings
The Little Wine Bottle of a Drunkard: A Shaped Poem in Ladino by A. Ben Guiat
Pangur Ban - The Monk and His Cat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two versions of the poem (in Gaelic and W. H. Auden's English translation) as well as artwork by Charles A. Jolly.

did.unittitle: Akavya Ben Mahalalel, Ethics of the Fathers, 3:1
Fritz Kredel: Selected Woodcuts, Tristan & Isolde
Fritz Kredel: Woodcuts for Fables of Berekhiah Ha-Nakdan
Fritz Kredel: Woodcuts from the Offenbacher Haggadah, 1927
Fritz Kredel: Aesop's Fables, Selected Drawings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One copy is the gift of Judith Kredel Brown, 2009.

did.unittitle: Fritz Kredel: Four Drawings for Poems of Heinrich Heine
Fritz Kredel: Six Woodcuts for Medieval Latin Lyrics
Fritz Kredel Woodcuts: Little Musgrave and Lady Barnard, May Collin and the Knight, Chevy Chase, Edward, Our Goodman, Robin Hood and the Monk
Giovanni Boccaccio The Decameron [with] Fritz Kredel Woodcuts
Fritz Kredel Drawings: Scenes from Opera
Proverbs F. Kredel
Season's Greetings
Confession: This Printer's Credo [from Micromegas by Voltaire]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes related correspondence from Arthur Graham.

did.unittitle: 3.1.1941 Zu allgemeiner Beachtung teile ich im Auftrage des Führers mit: ... M. Bormann
[3 Poems (Storm, Goethe, Eichendorff) with English Translations ("At My Desk," "Classified Ad," and "A Toast")]
Fäunsflötenlied (Faun's Flutesong) by Otto Julius Bierbaum (poem printed in English and German)
Fritz Kredel: Tristan und Isolde, Two Woodcuts (edition of 70)
Heinrich von Veldeke c. 1190 (poem printed in Germanic and English)
Should Old & Quaint s Be Forgot?
"Wagner's music is better than it sounds." — Mark Twain (Quotation illustrated by Fritz Kredel)
Correspondence & Ephemera
List of 10 works by Polyglot Press
